creator: royger@freebsd.org
text:
Do you think you could attempt to follow:

https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/developers-handbook/kerneldebug/#kerneldebug-online-ddb

And force entering KDB by pressing Ctrl+Alt+ESC when the kernel is stuck in order to attempt to get a trace?

I would like to understand where the kernel gets stuck, and why this seems to happen on some hardware only.

If that doesn't work I can try to provide a tweaked xen kernel that will inject a #GP to FreeBSD after a certain time, in order to enter KDB and print a trace.
